notes: CSA's product security program covers websites and services (cloudsecurityalliance.org, csachapter.io, star.watch, webfinger.io), software in CSA-managed GitHub organizations (CloudSecurityAlliance, CloudSecurityAlliance-Chatbots, CloudSecurityAlliance-DataSets, modelcontextprotocol-security, RiskRubric), and AI prompts and instructions published by CSA. The full program is documented in the csa-product-security repository. CSA does not run a bug bounty. Safe harbor applies to good-faith reporters. SLA: acknowledge within 5 business days, status updates every 30 days, remediation or disclosure within 90 days.

data.description: Machine-readable security contact and disclosure information per RFC 9116.
data.notes: Standard location at /.well-known/security.txt. Contains contact information, preferred languages, disclosure policy link, and expiration date.

match_nodes.description: CSA's vulnerability disclosure program — coordinated disclosure with safe harbor

data.description: Primary channel for reporting security vulnerabilities in CSA websites, services, software, and AI artifacts.
data.notes: Two reporting channels: (1) GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting (PVR) — preferred for software and repo-hosted AI artifacts; open the repo's Security tab and select Report a vulnerability. (2) Email security@cloudsecurityalliance.org — for websites, services, or non-repo AI artifacts; supports anonymous/pseudonymous submissions and PGP. CSA follows coordinated disclosure: acknowledge within 5 business days, status updates every 30 days, remediation or disclosure within 90 days. No bug bounty. Safe harbor for good-faith reporters.

contacts.note: GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting — preferred for software and repo-hosted AI artifacts
For websites, services, or non-repo AI artifacts. Supports anonymous/pseudonymous and PGP.
